input: I have a B2B SaaS tool for HR teams. Currently charging $99/month flat. Usage has grown 3x but revenue is flat. Should I switch to usage-based pricing? What are the risks?
criteria: directly addresses whether to switch (yes/no with reasoning, not just 'consider it')
correctly diagnoses the problem: usage 3x but revenue flat = wrong pricing model
explains the core trade-off of usage-based pricing (aligned incentives vs revenue unpredictability)
recommends a specific pricing structure or hybrid (not just 'usage-based')
names at least 2 concrete risks of switching
addresses how to handle existing customers during a transition
judge_prompt: You are evaluating a pricing strategy recommendation from an AI strategy assistant. Score 0-10. Full marks: clear yes/no on the switch, diagnosis of the flat-revenue-despite-growth problem, specific pricing structure proposed (not just 'usage-based' as a category), at least 2 named transition risks, existing customer handling addressed. Deduct 3 for no clear recommendation. Deduct 2 if no specific structure proposed. Deduct 1 per missing criterion.
